page_label: Drill — raw trust material in row (withdrawn)

rows.health_label: OS platform CA bundle is active and current; managed routes use system roots unless a custom CA bundle is declared by admin policy.
rows.local_continuity_label: Local editing, file operations, and non-TLS work are unaffected by OS root changes. Only routes using TLS against OS roots require revalidation after an OS bundle update.
rows.plain_language_summary: Row 'harden-os-keychain-trust-store:os_roots' (layer: os_roots) is withdrawn (reason: raw_trust_material_exposed): hard guardrail triggered.

rows.health_label: Custom CA bundle declared by admin policy is active; enterprise TLS routes validate against this bundle.
rows.local_continuity_label: Local editing, local file operations, and OS-root-only routes remain available. Only enterprise TLS routes pinned to the custom CA bundle require revalidation on bundle change.
rows.plain_language_summary: Row 'harden-os-keychain-trust-store:custom_ca_bundle' (layer: custom_ca_bundle) is withdrawn (reason: raw_trust_material_exposed): hard guardrail triggered.

rows.health_label: Pinned SSH host proofs are enrolled and match the current known-hosts for managed Git-over-SSH endpoints.
rows.local_continuity_label: Local editing and HTTPS-based Git routes remain available. Only Git-over-SSH sessions to managed endpoints require revalidation when a host proof changes.
rows.plain_language_summary: Row 'harden-os-keychain-trust-store:pinned_ssh_host_proof' (layer: pinned_ssh_host_proof) is withdrawn (reason: raw_trust_material_exposed): hard guardrail triggered.

rows.health_label: Client certificate material is enrolled in the OS keychain and accessible to managed mTLS routes.
rows.local_continuity_label: Local editing and non-mTLS routes remain available. Only routes requiring mutual TLS are blocked when client-certificate material becomes inaccessible or expires.
rows.plain_language_summary: Row 'harden-os-keychain-trust-store:client_certificate' (layer: client_certificate) is withdrawn (reason: raw_trust_material_exposed): hard guardrail triggered.

rows.health_label: Mirror trust roots for declared signed mirrors and air-gapped artifact repositories are active and verified.
rows.local_continuity_label: Local editing and OS-root TLS routes remain available. Only mirror-sync and air-gapped artifact routes require revalidation when a mirror trust root changes.
rows.plain_language_summary: Row 'harden-os-keychain-trust-store:mirror_trust_root' (layer: mirror_trust_root) is withdrawn (reason: raw_trust_material_exposed): hard guardrail triggered.

change_events.attribution_label: OS platform vendor updated the system CA bundle via a platform security update.
change_events.affected_sessions_label: Enterprise TLS routes using OS roots require revalidation; local editing and non-TLS work continue without interruption.
change_events.local_continuity_label: Local editing, file operations, search, and non-TLS routes continue unaffected. Enterprise TLS routes using OS roots will revalidate on the next connection attempt.
change_events.repair_action_label: Wait for the OS platform update to complete, then revalidate TLS routes against the updated OS root bundle. No user action is required unless a route fails after revalidation.

change_events.attribution_label: Admin policy bundle was updated with a new custom CA bundle for enterprise TLS routes.
change_events.affected_sessions_label: Enterprise TLS routes validated against the custom CA bundle require revalidation; all other routes and local work are unaffected.
change_events.local_continuity_label: Local editing and OS-root TLS routes remain fully available. Enterprise routes pinned to the custom CA bundle revalidate on the next connection attempt.
change_events.repair_action_label: Apply the updated custom CA bundle declared by the current managed policy, then revalidate enterprise TLS routes. Contact the workspace admin if revalidation fails.

change_events.attribution_label: A new SSH host proof was added by admin policy for a managed Git-over-SSH endpoint.
change_events.affected_sessions_label: New Git-over-SSH sessions to the enrolled managed endpoint are now protected by the pinned host proof; existing sessions are unaffected.
change_events.local_continuity_label: Local editing, HTTPS-based Git routes, and all non-SSH work continue unaffected. The new host proof only applies to future SSH sessions to the enrolled endpoint.
change_events.repair_action_label: No repair action is required; the new SSH host proof was added successfully.

change_events.attribution_label: Client certificate was renewed by admin policy; the previous certificate approached its expiry and was replaced with a fresh enrollment.
change_events.affected_sessions_label: Active mTLS sessions may need to re-present the renewed certificate on the next handshake; local editing and non-mTLS routes are unaffected.
change_events.local_continuity_label: Local editing, local file operations, and non-mTLS routes remain fully available. Only mTLS routes to managed endpoints require re-presentation of the renewed certificate on the next connection.
change_events.repair_action_label: The renewed client certificate has been enrolled in the OS keychain. Re-present the certificate on the next mTLS connection attempt. No user action is required if the OS keychain is unlocked.

change_events.attribution_label: Mirror trust root was refreshed via a signed mirror-sync transport; the admin-signed bundle was updated with a current mirror root.
change_events.affected_sessions_label: Mirror-sync and air-gapped artifact routes will revalidate against the updated mirror trust root on the next sync attempt; local editing is unaffected.
change_events.local_continuity_label: Local editing, OS-root TLS routes, and enterprise routes not using mirror trust roots remain fully available. Only mirror-sync and air-gapped artifact routes revalidate on the next sync.
change_events.repair_action_label: The mirror trust root has been refreshed via the signed mirror-sync transport. Mirror-sync and air-gapped artifact routes will revalidate on the next sync attempt. No user action is required.

defects.note: row does not exclude raw trust material (certificate bodies, private keys, fingerprints); packet is withdrawn
